details.unique_ids: 3/3 All task_ids are unique and non-empty

llm_grounding.reason: T1-T5 are grounded: each assignee (David, Maria, James) appears in the transcript; the described actions/objects match what is said (performance audit by 6/20; API spec by 6/30; dashboard wireframes/prototypes by 7/14; implement GraphQL endpoints by 8/1; QA test plan+automated suite with full coverage/sign-off by 8/15). Their source_span text clearly corresponds to the transcript statements. T6 is content-grounded (James sets up CI/CD pipeline integration for automated regression testing, due 7/18) and the assignee appears, but the provided source_span is truncated and its start/end positions cannot be verified against the transcript as given, so it is not reliably grounded to the cited span. No other hallucinations detected.

completeness.reason: Captured the main engineering/design/QA deliverables (performance audit, API spec, wireframes, implementation, test plan/suite, CI/CD). Missing explicit action items: (1) Linda Wu to perform data migration (scripts by 2025-07-25 and final validation by 2025-08-08), (2) Maria Lopez to conduct UI design review/final adjustments by 2025-08-22, (3) Sarah Chen to compile stakeholder review package and schedule executive demo/presentation by 2025-08-29, and (4) Sarah to reconvene/schedule progress check in two weeks (meeting follow-up).

accuracy.reason: T1–T5 are largely faithful to the transcript (assignees and deadlines align). Issues: T2 implies 'finalize' the spec; transcript says draft and get reviewed by June 30 (finalized is implied but not explicit). T4 assigns implementation to David Kim personally, while transcript indicates his backend team/two senior engineers will implement (assignee attribution is slightly off). T6 appears accurate but the source_span is truncated, reducing verifiability; also the exact deadline (July 18) matches transcript.

standardization.reason: Consistent structure with task_id/assignee/action/object/deadline and ISO date format. Actions are mostly concise and objects are specific enough. Minor standardization issues: some actions are compound ('Draft and finalize...') and T4's action/object could better reflect 'team implements' vs individual ownership. T6 has truncated source_span text, which is a formatting/traceability weakness.

semantic_plausibility.reason: Several edges are directionally wrong (e.g., T2->T1 should be T1->T2; T3->T2 should be T2->T3). T5->T6 is also reversed given the rationale (CI/CD before automated testing implies T6->T5 for the execution/sign-off portion). Multiple dependencies reference tasks not present in the action list (T7, T8), which makes those relationships unverifiable and undermines semantic coherence.

completeness.reason: Core intended dependencies are present in rationale, but many are encoded with the wrong direction, effectively breaking the graph’s ability to represent prerequisite structure. Additionally, tasks T7/T8 are missing entirely from the action list, so any dependencies involving them are incomplete by definition. If CI/CD is required for automated regression execution, there should be an explicit edge from T6 to the portion of T5 that depends on it (or split T5 into scaffolding vs execution).

dependency_types.reason: Labeling everything as "sequential" is overly coarse. Some are true prerequisite/finish-to-start constraints (audit before spec; spec before wireframes; wireframes+spec before implementation; implementation before full test execution), but others are better expressed as "enables" or partial overlap (T5 can start in parallel with T4; T6 enables automated regression runs). The type assignments are further compromised by reversed edge directions.

eval_report.comment: Good - Task basically completed, some dimensions have room for improvement
